info.description:
# Introduction

The Kushki API allows you to interact with all the information you need to integrate our payment solutions to your business. It uses API REST, so the URLs are resource-oriented, returns JSON encoded responses, and uses verbs, authentication, and standard HTTP response codes.

Before you can integrate the Kushki API, you need to obtain either sandbox or production credentials. To make requests, you have to authenticate each request with your business credentials. If you don't have credentials yet, see the basic steps to start implementing our solutions.

# Base URL

Production Base URL: [https://api.kushkipagos.com](https://api.kushkipagos.com)  
UAT (Sandbox) Base URL: [https://api-uat.kushkipagos.com](https://api-uat.kushkipagos.com)

# Authentication

The Kushki API uses API Keys to authenticate requests. You can view and manage your API Keys from the Kushki console.

There are two types of keys:

Public key: It allows you to request tokens for certain services as well as query certain information.  
Private key: It allows you to perform different operations for your commerce with our API. It should never be shared..  
You have to make all requests to our API using HTTPS and include your business credentials in the request headers to authenticate each interaction.

# Error Codes

Kushki uses HTTP response status codes to indicate the response of a request (successful or failed) to the API. In general:

Codes in the 2xx range indicate a successful response.  
Codes in the 4xx range indicate that the information sent is incorrect (e.g., some parameter required in the request is incorrect or missing).  
Codes in the 5xx range indicate a problem with the Kushki servers (these are rare).

post.description:
Request a card token that can later be used to charge a customer using the `charge` endpoint.

Important to consider: If you are using this option to request the token, be sure to comply with all the PCI requirements to handle card data on your servers.

> #### Token expiration > >Tokens will expire in 30 minutes and can be used only for a single transaction (even if the transaction was wrong). You will need to request a new token when it expires. > **Note: The token delivered by Kushki only encrypts and sends information. If you want to store the card information for future purchases, go to the [One-Click & Scheduled Payments](https://api-docs.kushkipagos.com/docs/online-payments/one-click-and-scheduled-payments) section.**
### 3D Secure (3DS) authentication* > #### Product in beta version 🔐👨‍💻 > > We are working on our beta version. Stay tuned for its official release! You can also contact your account manager for more information. To authenticate a card through 3D Secure, in addition to the information required to generate a token, the fields listed below must be submitted | **PROPERTY** | **POSSIBLE VALUES** | **DESCRIPTION** | |----------------|---------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | authValidation | `url` `iframe` | Define the desired integration type for 3DS authentication. Use `url` for redirection or `iframe `for embedding. | | callbackUrl | string | Callback where the 3D Secure authentication response will be sent. | If the transaction triggers a 3D Secure authentication rule on the merchant, in addition to the token, the fields listed below will be returned | **PROPERTY** | **POSSIBLE VALUES** | **DESCRIPTION** | |---------------|---------------------|----------------------------------------------| | url | string | URL to be used for 3D Secure authentication. | | secureService | `3dsecure` | Indicates the transaction authentication service. | | secureId | string | Security ID for the transaction. | **Example** ```json { "token": "sBkQ7F110000tI1HVq116862fd5Ah3mG", "url": "https://uat-auth.kushkipagos.com?token=sBkQ7F110000tI1HVq116862fd5Ah3mG&merchantId=306cb10581bb4acb9a2bfc77e163c482&bin=NDM0OTAwMzA=&callbackUrl=https://www.kushkipagos.com/callback/&isSandbox=false", "secureService": "3dsecure", "secureId": "1f5584db-0c5b-c729-a19c-6eb0283ca448" } ``` In the `url` field will be the url that will need to be displayed to the cardholder for 3D Secure authentication, if necessary. For more detailed information, visit the [3D Secure API authentication](https://docs.kushki.com/mx/card-payments/3d-secure/3ds-via-api) guide. For more information about 3D Secure, see [**Accept payments with 3DS**](https://docs.kushki.com/mx/card-payments/3d-secure/3ds-overview). > **NOTE:** To enable 3DS in test mode in your merchant, it is required to have an additional configuration performed by Kushki. #### callbackUrl The callback function will let you know if the 3D Secure authentication was successful or if there were any problems during the process. This url will be called automatically after authenticating, through a 301 redirect by making a GET request to the url sent in the `callbackUrl` field, sending the parameters of the response in the url (path parameters). > **Note**: The boolean parameter sucess will indicate whether the transaction was successfully authenticated (`true`) or if there was a problem with 3D Secure authentication (`false`). If it is `true`, you can proceed to make the charge. If `false`, the cardholder will have up to 3 attempts to retry authentication before the transaction is declined. | **PROPERTY** | **TYPE** | **DESCRIPTION** | |--------------|----------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | success | boolean | Indicates whether 3D Secure authentication was successful or not. | | token | string | Token returned by Kushki to be used when making a charge in case 3D Secure authentication has been successful. | | message | string | In case there is a problem during authentication, the reason for the problem will be returned here. | ##### Example of a successful authentication callback ``` https://www.tutienda.com/?success=true&token=cfa0bfec88324bd7a5c6c1ad9135a846 ``` ##### Example of a callback with authentication problem ``` https://www.tutienda.com/?success=false&message=Error%20en%20la%20validación%20de%203DS&token=cfa0bfec88324bd7a5c6c1ad9135a846 ```

post.description:
### Deferred charges If you perform a deferred charge with credentials of an Ecuadorian, you have to set the `deferred` object that has the `creditType`, `graceMonths` and `months`. #### How to obtain the available deferred options? As soon as your customer’s card number is entered, you must use the method [Request deferred options](https://api-docs.kushkipagos.com/docs/online-payments/card/operations/get-a-card-v-1-deferred) to verify if there are deferred payment options for the BIN of the card entered by the user.
In response to this call, you will receive the name of the deferral, the type (corresponds to the `creditType` when executing the charge), the available months and the months of grace, as shown in the following example response. ```json { name: "Diferido con intereses", type: "002", months: ["3", "6"], monthsOfGrace: ["1", "2", "3"] } ``` Some types of deferrals that may be returned in the response are: * Diferido con meses de gracia sin intereses. * Diferido con meses de gracia con intereses. * Diferido cuota fija con intereses. * Diferido cuota fija sin intereses. * Diferido con intereses. * Diferido sin intereses. ### Do you have your own 3DS authentication engine?* Make sure you include the `threeDomainSecure` object in your request. > **Endpoint time out: 29 sec**

post.description:
Validate the OTP provided by the client with the `secureId` from the token request. The client has 5 minutes and also 3 attempts to validate the OTP with the same `secureId`.

## Sandbox

Kushki allows simulating an approved OTP validation. Use the following values depending on the currency that belongs to your credentials:

* USD: `155`

Any other OTP value will result in a declined validation.

delete.description:
Refund a transaction identified by its `ticketNumber`. The `ticketNumber` is provided when a charge or a deferred charge is successful. Remember that the refund process is semi-automatic, so the confirmation you are receiving is to confirm you that the process has begun successfully.

### Partial refunds

For partial refunds it is necessary to send the `amount` object.

:::danger
#### Important 
Refunds must be requested within **30 days to 6 months** of the original transaction date, depending on the processor. Requests submitted after this period will be automatically rejected.

> **Only one (1) partial refund is allowed per transaction.** If a second partial refund is attempted on the same charge, the request will be automatically denied, even if there is a remaining balance.
:::

#### Partial refund request example

```json
{
  "fullResponse": "v2",
  "amount": {
    "subtotalIva": 8737,
    "subtotalIva0": 9976,
    "ice": 2099,
    "iva": 8967,
    "currency": "COP"
  }
}
```

post.description:
Create a recurring charge with a `token` provided by Kushki which represents the customer's credit card.

### Payment Execution

Kushki processes __recurring charges to the registered card automatically__. Payments will be executed and repeated according to the amount and period indicated in the subscription.

The billing logic starts running every day __from 6am GMT-5__. Make sure that the subscription was created before this time if you want the first payment to be executed on the same day of registration. Alternatively, your customer can register his/her card some days in advance, before Kushki executes the first charge. When creating a subscription, you must indicate the __start date `startDate`__.

#### Retries

If a payment is rejected, Kushki has a retry logic to try to recover the charge. By default, __there are 3 retries for 3 days in a row from the payment day originally defined in `startDate`__. For example, for a monthly subscription with start date: `10-01-2021`, with a rejection on `10-02-2021`, payment will be retried until `13-02-2021`, 3 times each day, for a total of 9 payment attempts.

You can modify the retry days for a subscription and also define specific charging days.

If the __last charge attempt was declined__, we may send you a notification via WebHook informing you about it. In these cases, we recommend you to contact the card owner and offer him/her a set of alternatives for paying the fee that could not be charged; for example, changing the registered credit card and generating a [charge on demand](https://api-docs.kushkipagos.com/docs/online-payments/one-click-and-scheduled-payments%2Foperations%2Fcreate-a-subscription-v-1-card-1) only for one time. Kushki will continue executing automatic charges over the following periods.

##### Customize retry logic

You can customize the collection retry logic by submitting the `retryConfiguration` object as follows:

1. `retryType`:"schedule". If you choose schedule, you can specify in the `value` field you can specify in `value` how often the collection will be reattempted.
In the next example, the collection will be reattempted 3 times per day every 2 days for the entire month.
```json
{
   "retryConfiguration":{
      "retryType":"schedule",
      "value":[2]
   }
}
```

2. `retryType`:"fixed". Send the specific days of the month to retry. Ex. 10th, 20th, 30th.
  In the following example, the charge will only be reattempted 3 times a day, on the 10th, 20th and 30th of the corresponding month.
 ```json
{
   "retryConfiguration":{
      "retryType":"fixed",
      "value":[10,20,30]
   }
}
```
>Send the `retryType` as `fixed` if you do not want to retry collection for the entire month.
If the __last charge attempt was declined__, we may send you a notification via WebHook informing you about it. In these cases, we recommend you to contact the card owner and offer them a set of alternatives for paying the fee that could not be charged; for example, changing the registered credit card and generating an [One-click payment](https://docs.kushki.com/mx/en/one-time-payments/card/on-demand) only for one time. Kushki will continue executing automatic charges over the following periods.

<!-- theme: warning -->
> #### Important!
>
> When customizing retries it should be noted that once the first collection is initiated, if it fails and retries are initiated, any updates to retries will be applied until the next subscription period (weekly, monthly, yearly).

#### Periodicity

You can choose the frequency of recurring charges by submitting any of these values in the required `periodicity` field:

* **daily**: every 1 day.
* **weekly**: every 7 days.
* **biweekly**: every 15 days.
* **monthly**: every 30 days.
* **threefortnights**: every six weeks or 42 days.
* **bimonthly**: every 60 days.
* **quarterly**: every 90 days.
* **fourmonths**: every 120 days.
* **halfyearly**: every 180 days
* **yearly**: every 360 days.
* **custom**: for [one click payments](https://api-docs.kushkipagos.com/docs/online-payments/one-click-and-scheduled-payments%2Foperations%2Fcreate-a-subscription-v-1-card-1).

### Do you have your own 3DS authentication engine?

<!-- theme: warning -->
> #### Product in beta version  🔐👨‍💻
>
> We are working on our beta version. Stay tuned for its official release! You can also contact your account manager for more information.

Make sure you include the `threeDomainSecure` object in your request.

post.description:
Returns a paginated list of chargebacks associated with the authenticated merchant.

#### Filters object
Use the `filters` object to narrow results by `transaction_date`, `request_date`, `chargeback_status`, `country_name`, `chargeback_type`, `ticket_code`, or `chargeback_ticket_code`.

#### Time Filter
The `time` filter is required and works as follows:
 - Send either `transaction_date` **or** `request_date`
 - Sending both returns an error.
 - The maximum allowed date window is **3 months**.
#### Fields array
The `fields` array allows you to request additional fields beyond the default response set. If `fields` is omitted, only the standard fields are returned.

> **Limit:** The maximum allowed value for `page_size` in this version is **100 records** per page.

post.description:
Initiates the asynchronous generation of a chargeback export file.

Upon receiving the request, the system immediately returns a `200 OK` response with a unique `id`. Once the file has been generated and stored, Kushki sends a `POST` notification to each URL configured in the `webhooks` array, including the download link and its expiration timestamp.

The request body follows the same structure as `POST /data/v1/chargebacks/search`, with the addition of the `webhooks` field.

> **Note:** The download URL is an S3 pre-signed URL valid for **4 hours** from the moment of generation. Check `expiration_timestamp` in the webhook notification before attempting the download.

#### Webhook security

To verify that a notification was sent by Kushki and not a third party, validate the following headers included in every webhook call:

| Header | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `X-Kushki-Id` | Unix timestamp (13 digits, milliseconds) of when the notification was sent. |
| `X-Kushki-Signature` | HMAC SHA256 signature of `private-merchant-id\|request\|X-Kushki-Id`. The signing secret is the merchant's own `private-merchant-id`. |

To validate the signature on your side, compute:

```
HMAC-SHA256({private-merchant-id}, "{private-merchant-id}|{request}|{X-Kushki-Id}")
```

And compare the result with the value received in `X-Kushki-Signature`. If they match, the notification is authentic.

under it, filtered by a date range.

Use the `start` and `end` query parameters to define the billing period you want

get.description:
Retrieve a paginated list of transactions for a specific merchant using the **Get Transaction List v2 API**. This version supports both card-present and card-not-present transactions, as well as filtering by pay-ins and pay-outs. Transactions are returned in descending order, with the most recent ones appearing first.

>  **To Keep in mind:**
>*   Transactions can only be queried through this endpoint `90 seconds` after their creation.
>*   The maximum number of transactions you can query per request is `600`.
>* 	In case the `limit` parameter is not included in the request, the maximum number of transations that will be returned is `100`.

post.description:
Create a smartLink that can later be shared to a customer in order to complete the payment process.

In the form you can include 6 different type of items:
1) Input: Useful to create tags so customers can include name, email and other important information for the business.
2) Select: Useful to select from a list of available options.
3) Date: Shows a calendar if you need the customer to select a specific date.
4) Area: Useful to enter multiple lines of text.
5) Checkbox: Useful to let a customer select one or more options from a limited list of choices.
6) Radio: Allows the customer to choose only one of a limited list of choices.

- Unique: For one time payment
- Subscription: To create a recurring subscription. Only available with card
- Mixed: To include the first two choices in the same smartlink

numberOfFees.description:
Number of periods the subscription is going to be charged.
- Use `0` for an undefined number of charges. The subscription has to be canceled manually
- If it is different than 0, the subscription is going to be canceled automatically once its charged the number of periods specified

subscriptionDay.description:
- Use `0`, so that the subscription is charged in the next 24 hours
- If it is different than 0, the subscription is going to be charged on that particular day of the month

post.description:
:::info Availability
This functionality is available for the following models: ☑ Acquirer ☑ Aggregator
:::

Retrieves merchant settlement records on demand. Supports two query modes:

- **By date range** — include `startDate` and `endDate` in the request body to retrieve a paginated list of settlement records for the specified period.
- **By ticket number** — include a `ticketNumber` in the request body to retrieve the settlement record associated with a specific transaction. When using this mode, include the `switch` query parameter to specify the payment channel. Returns a single record in the `data` array.

## Default Behavior (Date Range Mode)

- `page` defaults to `1`.
- `limit` defaults to `100` records per page.

## Date Range Limit

:::warning 6-Month Lookback Limit
Queries are limited to a maximum lookback of **6 months** from the current date.

Requests with a `startDate` older than 6 months will **not return results** — no error is thrown.

Make sure your `startDate` falls within the allowed window before sending the request.
:::

threeDomainSecure.title: threeDomainSecure
threeDomainSecure.description:
Send this object if you have your own 3DS authentication engine.

**VISA (Required)**:
* cavv
* eci
* specificationVersion

**Mastercard (Required)**:
* directoryServerTransactionID
* eci
* ucaf
* specificationVersion
* collectionIndicator

**Electronic Commerce Indicator (ECI) allowed values:**

* Visa
  * eci: `05` and `06` represent a secure transaction.
  * eci: `07` represents a risky transaction. For processing risky transactions, also set the `acceptRisk` field to `true`. **By sending `true`, in the `acceptRisk` field, merchants will assume responsibility in case of chargebacks.**
* Mastercard:
  * eci: `01` and `02` represent a secure transaction.
  * eci: `00` represents a risky transaction. For processing risky transactions, also set the `acceptRisk` field to `true`. **By sending `true`, in the `acceptRisk` field, merchants will assume responsibility in case of chargebacks.**

<!-- theme: warning -->
> #### Important notice about support for version 1 of 3D Secure!
>**NOTE**: Support for 3D Secure 1.0.2 and related technology ended in October 2022. Merchants will need to migrate to version 2 of the protocol to avoid any impact on their transactions.

cavv.description: **Required only for Visa.** Cardholder Authentication Verification Value. For Visa Secure transactions, the issuer's access control generates a CAVV. **Base 64 encoded.**

eci.description: The Electronic Commerce Indicator (ECI) is a value returned by the directory servers (i.e. Visa, Mastercard, JCB and American Express) indicating the result of the attempted authentication.

specificationVersion.description:
3DS protocol version to implement

**NOTE: Support for 3D Secure 1.0.2 and related technology ended in October 2022. Merchants will need to migrate to version 2 of the protocol to avoid any impact on their transactions.**

acceptRisk.description: Indicates whether risky transactions are required to be processed or not. For processing high-risk transactions, set this parameter to `true` and send `07` in the case of Visa or `00` for Mastercard in the `eci` field. **By sending `true` in this field, merchants will assume responsibility for chargebacks.**

ucaf.description: **Required only for Mastercard.** Universal Cardholder Authentication Field. **It´s the equivalent to the CAVV. Base 64 encoded.**

collectionIndicator.description:
**Required only for Mastercard**

* 0 (for ECI 00) = 3DS authentication failed or could not be attempted, this may be due to technical problems, the user entered the authentication code wrong, etc.
* 1 (for ECI 01) = The issuer is not ready, however the transaction has a liability shift because the merchant did request 3DS.
* 2 (for ECI 02) = Transaction authenticated by the issuer with liability shift.

directoryServerTransactionID.description: **Only required for Mastercard.** Transaction ID of the 3D Secure provider's directory server. Applies only for version 2 of 3DS.

retryConfiguration.description: Use this object if you want to configure retries different than default

retryType.description:
**Schedule:** Number of days to retry starting at `startDate`. **Ex.** 5 days in a row.

**Fixed:** Specific days of the month to retry. **Ex.** 10th, 20th, 30th.

f39.description: Generic response codes from card Visa or Mastercard. It could be received both for approved or declined transactions

ChargebackItem.description:
Represents a chargeback record. The set of fields returned depends on whether the `fields` array is included in the request.

Fields listed under **Default fields** are always returned. Fields listed under **Optional fields** are only included when explicitly requested via the `fields` parameter.

id.description: Unique identifier of the chargeback record.

chargeback_ticket_code.description: Chargeback ticket number. May contain the value `NO_INFO` when the data is not available.

merchant_name.description: Name of the merchant or branch associated with the transaction.

chargeback_status.description: Current status of the chargeback in the Kushki system.

reason_code.description: Chargeback reason code as defined by the payment network. In some cases only the description (`reason_description`) is available.

request_amount.description: Amount requested by the merchant in the original sale transaction.

transaction_date.description: Date and time when the original sale was executed (ISO 8601 UTC).

request_date.description: Date when the chargeback was reported to the card networks or processors.

deadline_representation_date.description: **Calculated field.** Deadline for submitting representation evidence. Calculated as `request_date + 15 calendar days`.

deadline_resolution_date.description: **Calculated field.** Maximum deadline for case resolution. Calculated as `request_date + 120 calendar days`.

risk_level.description:
**Calculated field.** Urgency level of the chargeback at query time, based on the number of days remaining until `deadline_representation_date`.

| Value | Condition | |-------|-----------| | `HIGH` | ≤ 5 days until deadline | | `MEDIUM` | 6 – 15 days until deadline | | `LOW` | > 15 days until deadline |

ticket_code.description: Sale transaction ticket number linked to this chargeback. Use this field to correlate the chargeback with its original sale in Kushki.

operation_id.description: Unique code of the chargeback request.

transaction_reference.description: Reference number of the original sale transaction.

merchant_code.description: Unique code of the merchant or branch (Merchant ID).

approved_transaction_amount.description: Approved amount of the original sale transaction.

transaction_type.description: Transaction type of the original sale. Expected value — `SALE`.

transaction_status.description: Final status of the original sale transaction.

transaction_date_source.description: Transaction date as reported by the card networks or processors.

notification_status.description: Indicates whether the chargeback notification was sent to the merchant's registered email addresses.

documentation_reception_date.description: Date when the chargeback documentation was received.

issuing_delivery_date.description: Date when the chargeback representation was initiated.

create_timestamp.description: Unix timestamp (13 digits, milliseconds) of when Kushki registered the chargeback request in its system.

update_timestamp.description: Unix timestamp (13 digits, milliseconds) of the last update to the chargeback record in Kushki.

processor_name.description: Name of the processor that handled the original sale transaction.

card_country_name.description: Country of the card used in the transaction.

security_service.description: Security service the sale transaction passed through (e.g., 3DS, fraud scoring).

security_message.description: Response message from the security service.

last_four_digit_code.description: Last four digits of the card used in the transaction.

source.description: Source through which the chargeback was reported to Kushki.

message.description: Technical detail of the validation error.

Message.example: User is not authorized to access this resource because no identity-based policy allows the execute-api:Invoke action

example.Message: User is not authorized to access this resource because no identity-based policy allows the execute-api:Invoke action

ErrorResponse500.description: Error response for internal server error (500).

example.message: An unexpected error occurred. Please try again later.

StatusComponent.title: StatusComponent
StatusComponent.description: Represents a single platform component and its current operational status, along with uptime metrics for the reported date range.

name.description: Display name of the platform component.

description.description: Optional description of the component. Returns `null` when no description has been set.

status.description:
Current operational status of the component.

* `operational`: Functioning normally with no known issues.
* `degraded_performance`: Reduced performance; component remains functional.
* `partial_outage`: Some functionality unavailable for a subset of users.
* `major_outage`: Component is completely unavailable or severely impacted.

position.description: Display order of the component within the platform's status page. Lower values appear first.

group.description: Indicates whether this component acts as a group container for child components.

showcase.description: Indicates whether this component is highlighted on the public status page.

only_show_if_degraded.description: When `true`, this component is only visible on the status page if it is experiencing an incident.

start_date.description: The date from which this component started tracking uptime, in `YYYY-MM-DD` format. Returns an empty string if not set.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the component was created.

updated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the last update to this component's record.

range_start.description: Start of the reporting period for uptime metrics, in `YYYY-MM-DD` format.

range_end.description: End of the reporting period for uptime metrics, in `YYYY-MM-DD` format.

uptime_percentage.description: Uptime percentage for the component during the reporting period (`range_start` to `range_end`). Value between `0` and `100`.

major_outage.description: Number of major outage incidents recorded during the reporting period.

partial_outage.description: Number of partial outage incidents recorded during the reporting period.

SettlementDateRangeRequest.title: SettlementDateRangeRequest
SettlementDateRangeRequest.description: Request body for querying settlement records by date range.

startDate.description: Start of the settlement period to query, in `YYYY-MM-DD` format.

endDate.description: End of the settlement period to query, in `YYYY-MM-DD` format.

page.description: Page number to retrieve. Use alongside `limit` for pagination. **Default:** `1`.

limit.description: Maximum number of records to return per page. **Default:** `100`.

SettlementTicketRequest.title: SettlementTicketRequest
SettlementTicketRequest.description: Request body for querying a settlement record by ticket number. Returns a single record in the `data` array.

ticketNumber.description: Ticket number of the transaction whose settlement record you want to retrieve. This value is returned as `ticketNumber` in charge and capture responses. When this field is present, the `switch` query parameter is required.

SettlementResponse.title: SettlementResponse
SettlementResponse.description: Settlement records matching the query. When querying by `ticketNumber`, `data` contains exactly one element.

data.description: List of settlement records matching the query. Contains a single element when querying by `ticketNumber`.

pagination.description: Pagination metadata for the result set.

limit.description: Maximum number of records returned per page.

total.description: Total number of settlement records available for the queried period.

totalPages.description: Total number of pages available based on the current `limit`.

SettlementRecord.title: SettlementRecord
SettlementRecord.description: Individual settlement record for a processed transaction.

payment_date.description: Date on which the settlement payment was made to the merchant, in `YYYY-MM-DD` format.

day.description: Day of the month of the transaction creation date.

month.description: Month of the transaction creation date.

country.description: Country where the transaction was processed.

processor_type.description: Processing model under which the transaction was handled (e.g., `AGGREGATOR_FORMAL`, `ACQUIRER`).

credential_alias.description: Alias assigned to the credential used for the transaction.

created.description: Date on which the transaction was created, in `YYYY-MM-DD` format.

ticket_number.description: Unique ticket number assigned to the transaction by Kushki.

sale_ticket_number.description: Ticket number of the original sale transaction. Populated for voids and refunds; empty for sale transactions.

payment_method.description: Payment method used for the transaction.

buy_order.description: Merchant-provided order reference for the transaction.

document_number.description: Document number associated with the transaction, if applicable.

bin_card.description: First 6 digits of the card number (BIN).

card_type.description: Type of card used for the transaction.

foreign_card.description: Indicates whether the card was issued outside the merchant's country.

number_of_months.description: Number of deferred months applied to the transaction. `"0"` for non-deferred transactions.

issuing_bank.description: Name of the bank that issued the card.

approval_code.description: Authorization code returned by the issuer upon approval.

cash_pin.description: Cash PIN associated with the transaction, if applicable.

payment_point.description: Payment point identifier, if applicable (used for cash transactions).

min_fee_amount.description: Minimum fee amount applied to the transaction.

approved_transaction_amount.description: Total amount approved for this transaction in the transaction currency.

iva_value.description: VAT amount applied to the transaction.

ice_value.description: ICE tax amount applied to the transaction (Impuesto a Consumos Especiales).

variable_fee.description: Variable fee amount charged by Kushki for this transaction.

variable_percentage.description: Variable fee rate applied, expressed as a percentage.

static_amount.description: Fixed fee amount charged per transaction.

kushki_commission.description: Total Kushki commission amount charged for this transaction (variable fee + fixed fee).

iva_kushki_commission.description: VAT applied to the Kushki commission.

kushki_amount.description: Total amount retained by Kushki (commission + VAT on commission).

metadata.description: Additional metadata associated with the transaction, if provided at charge time.

fraud_retention.description: Amount retained for fraud prevention purposes.

adjustment.description: Manual adjustment amount applied to the settlement record.

wallet.description: Amount associated with wallet operations, if applicable.

fund_release.description: Amount released from a previously held fund.

commission_msi.description: Commission amount applied for months without interest (MSI) deferred payments.

iva_msi.description: VAT applied to the MSI commission.

pay_amount.description: Net amount to be paid to the merchant after deducting all fees and retentions (`approved_transaction_amount` minus `kushki_amount` and other deductions).

observation.description: Additional observation or note associated with the settlement record.

SubscriptionTransactionsResponse.title: SubscriptionTransactionsResponse
SubscriptionTransactionsResponse.description: Full details of a subscription, including card and plan metadata, plus the list of transactions charged within the requested date range.

active_indicator.description: Indicates whether the subscription is currently active.

amount_object.description: Recurring charge amount breakdown configured for this subscription.

ice.description: ICE tax amount (Impuesto a Consumos Especiales). `0` if not applicable.

subtotalIva.description: Subtotal subject to VAT. `0` if not applicable.

subtotalIva0.description: Subtotal not subject to VAT. Represents the total amount when no taxes apply.

bin_info_object.description: BIN (Bank Identification Number) metadata for the card registered to the subscription.

bin.description: First 6 digits of the registered card number.

alpha3.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the card's issuing country.

name.description: Full name of the card's issuing country.

type.description: Card type as reported by the BIN database.

originalBinFullLength.description: Full BIN including extended digits (8-digit BIN where available).

validBin8.description: Indicates whether the extended 8-digit BIN is valid and was resolved successfully.

card_holder_name.description: Full name of the cardholder as stored in the subscription.

card_type.description: Type of card registered to the subscription.

contact_details_object.description: Contact information of the subscription owner.

create_timestamp.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) of when the subscription was created.

day_of_month.description: Day of the month configured for the recurring charge. Returns `-2` for custom (on-demand) subscriptions.

day_of_week.description: Day of the week configured for weekly charges. Returns `"-"` when not applicable.

expiry_month.description: Expiration month of the registered card (MM format).

expiry_year.description: Expiration year of the registered card (YY format).

last_charge_timestamp.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) of the most recent charge executed for this subscription.

last_four_digit_code.description: Last four digits of the card registered to the subscription.

merchant_code.description: Unique identifier of the merchant who owns the subscription.

merchant_country_name.description: Country of the merchant associated with the subscription.

metadata_object.description: Custom key-value metadata attached to the subscription at creation time.

month.description: Month configured for the recurring charge in text format. Returns `"-"` when not applicable.

periodicity_type.description: Frequency at which the subscription is charged. Use `custom` for on-demand subscriptions.

plan_name.description: Name of the plan associated with the subscription.

provider_name.description: Name of the payment provider processing the subscription.

reference_transaction_code.description: Reference code of the initial validation transaction used to register the card.

start_timestamp.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) of the subscription's configured start date.

subscription_code.description: Unique identifier of the subscription. Same value as `subscriptionId`.

transactions.description: List of transactions charged under this subscription within the requested date range.

SubscriptionTransaction.title: SubscriptionTransaction
SubscriptionTransaction.description: Details of a single transaction executed under a subscription.

_id.description: Internal unique identifier of the transaction record.

acquirer_bank_name.description: Name of the acquiring bank that processed the transaction.

approval_code.description: Authorization code returned by the issuer upon approval.

approved_transaction_amount.description: Total amount approved for this transaction.

bin_code.description: BIN (Bank Identification Number) of the card used in this transaction.

card_country_code.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code of the card's issuing country.

card_country_name.description: Full name of the card's issuing country.

card_holder_name.description: Name of the cardholder as it appears in the transaction record.

card_type.description: Type of card used for this transaction.

contact_detail.description: Contact information of the cardholder at the time of the transaction.

contact_email.description: Primary contact email for this transaction.

core_source_name.description: Internal identifier of the Kushki core system that processed the transaction.

country_name.description: Country where the transaction was processed.

create_timestamp.description: Unix timestamp (milliseconds) of when the transaction was created.

foreign_card_indicator.description: Indicates whether the card used is issued outside the merchant's country.

ice_value.description: ICE tax amount applied to this transaction.

issuing_bank_name.description: Name of the bank that issued the card.

iva_value.description: VAT amount applied to this transaction.

kushki_info_origin_type.description: Origin type of the transaction within the Kushki platform. For subscription charges, this value is `SUBSCRIPTION`.

last_four_digit_code.description: Last four digits of the card used for this transaction.

merchant_code.description: Unique identifier of the merchant who processed the transaction.

merchant_name.description: Display name of the merchant who processed the transaction.

metadata_object.description: Additional metadata associated with this transaction, as provided at charge time. May include fraud prevention data (`fraudData`), external identifiers, and platform-specific fields.

amount.description: Amount sent in the metadata context.

externalId.description: External identifier provided by the merchant.

fraudData.description: Fraud prevention data submitted with the transaction.

payment_brand_name.description: Name of the card brand used (e.g., Visa, Mastercard).

payment_method_type.description: Payment method type used for this transaction.

payment_submethod_type.description: Payment sub-method type used for this transaction.

prepaid_indicator.description: Indicates whether the card used is a prepaid card.

processor_name.description: Name of the payment processor that handled the transaction.

processor_type.description: Processing model under which the transaction was handled (e.g., `aggregator_formal`, `acquirer`).

reference_transaction_code.description: UUID-based reference code for this transaction.

request_amount.description: Amount requested for this transaction before any adjustments.

response_code.description: Processor response code. `"000"` indicates a successful authorization.

response_description.description: Human-readable description of the processor response.

subscription_code.description: Identifier of the subscription this transaction belongs to.

subscription_metadata_object.description: Metadata stored at the subscription level, associated with this transaction.

subscription_trigger_type.description: Indicates how the charge was triggered. `onDemand` for one-click payments; `scheduled` for automatic recurring charges.

subtotal_iva0_amount.description: Subtotal not subject to VAT for this transaction.

subtotal_iva_amount.description: Subtotal subject to VAT for this transaction.

sync_mode_type.description: Processing mode of the transaction (`online` or `offline`).

ticket_code.description: Ticket number assigned to this transaction by Kushki.

transaction_code.description: Unique transaction identifier assigned by Kushki.

PrivateMerchantId1.description: Merchant's private credential. Obtained from the Kushki dashboard.
PrivateMerchantId1.name: private-merchant-id
